answer:
Each picture element has 10 equally likely brightness levels, so the information per element is \(\log_2 10 \approx 3.32\) bits. With 300,000 elements per frame and 30 frames per second, the total bit rate is \(300,000 \times 30 \times 3.32 \approx 29.88\) Mbps. Using the Shannon-Hartley theorem with SNR = 1000, bandwidth \(B = \frac{C}{\log_2(1+1000)} \approx \frac{29.88 \times 10^6}{9.97} \approx 3.0\) MHz.

\boxed{G}
